entry.title: Error in SAP BTP after Custom UI5 app deploy
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing issue while running custom Fiori UI5 app in BTP CF. It’s running fine in BAS preview but showing internal server error message after deploying the UI5 code in BTP CF.  Please suggest how to resolve it.

Error message in log file in BTP CF:

GET request to /7fc513a7-ec39-4d08-a074-c6a6f4933e81.comcfnote.comcfnote-0.0.1 completed with status 500 xs-app.json/routes/5: Missing required property: source

 

Thanks and Regards,

 Jyoti Prakash Das

Regards,
Saravanan

entry.title: Using Nio Protocol in Tomcat
entry.content: We are upgrading from Hybris 2205.22 to 2205.30 version. Since "org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11Protocol" which was used  earlier is removed, "org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11NioProtocol" is this the right one to replace

entry.title: Re: Master Data Management: The Foundation for Modern Enterprise Data Architecture
entry.content: Many Thanks, issue we are facing Data Governance (Informatica stack) we are running at enterprise level outside S4HANA /SAP and similar MDM we are running outside, now if we are using MDG + enterprise DG + Enterprise MDM how can we make sure these systems are synchronized from Stewardship, data quality and mastering pointing of view, rather than this becomes silos . hope you understand the question.

entry.title: Re: groovyc errors on ant clean all with SAP Commerce 2205
entry.content:
Do not paste the properties in your local.properties blindly
Check your error.
put only those extensions which are giving the error while ant all.

entry.title: Re: No batch input data for screen SAPMSSY0 0120 error while running BDC in back ground mode
entry.content: thank you so much!

entry.title: Release Assessment and Scope Dependency for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: What is new in 2025
entry.content:
Introduction

To provide our customers of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with disruption free upgrades and zero day 1 impact, we have been tirelessly improving release assessment and scope dependency tool to meet the latest customer demands.

We have implemented several changes and enhancements in the release assessment and scope dependency tool. These modifications enable us to proactively inform our customers of all Day 1 impacts relevant to their usage.

Keep watching this space for more updates on release assessment and scope dependency coming in 2025.

Applications

With the 2502 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the what’s new viewer have introduced a new attribute called ‘Action’. This was introduced to address, which tasks that are communicated via what’s new, need to be focused/prioritized so that customers could fulfil the required actions in time. This will ensure that the urgency of the tasks are communicated appropriately.

The attribute ‘Action have the following values:

  • “Critical – Immediate Action Required” - Classification for all changes that cause a day-1 impact for the customer
  • “Action/Review Required” - Classification for all changes that improve an existing process but does not cause an immediate disruption while working with the affected functionality.
  • “Innovation” = Classification for all changes that enable a new functionality or process. Customers can decide not to use the innovation or to postpone the usage.
  • “Information” = Classification for changes with minor impact, for example, structural changes of the documentation, or changed shipment cycles.

Release assessment and scope dependency have adapted itself to accommodate these changes and the application tile have been redesigned as follows. This ensures that release assessment and scope dependency communicates the changes with appropriate urgency to our customers.

New Features

We have also consolidated the ‘New Features’ tile to include the number of features delivered in a particular release, to ease the consumption of the tool.

Conclusion

We are continuously trying to add more features to the tool so that upgrades are hassle free and our customer can derive more value out of their SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation.

You can go thru our FAQ blog if you have any questions regarding release assessment and scope dependency.

entry.title: Re: Error ID: 016 (Pmnt methods for this run are not spec...
entry.content: Pmnt methods for this run are not specified in master record or in item

entry.title: Re: Horizon Theme and Templates for Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud

Hello @OzanCavusoglu,

You could open your SAC tenant with SAP horizon theme by adding "?sap-theme=sap_horizon" between "app.html" and "#" in your tenant url:

Best regards

Lothar

entry.title: Re: Winning together: SAP Signavio Process Explorer and SAP Signavio Process Navigator join forces
entry.content:
Dear Summitt12,

we're currently revising the Product branding and Product strategy for more clarity.

Best  regards

entry.title: Re: Product Substitutions
entry.content: Hello Jurgen, Thanks for taking your time to go through the article. To answer your query, The Product / Location Substitution would get triggered only on ATP run on checking of availability and deficits. Incase the substitution material is maintained for the original/ requested material and if the substitution material is available then during case of orginal/ requested material is unavailable then during those cases, substitution material is proposed and displayed during 'Review Availability Check Results (RACR)'. On acceptance would get processed for next steps. But the substitution logic doesnt kick-up until ATP check is not done. Hope this explaination clarifies your query.

Arvind

entry.title: Re: UI5 Building Blocks: How to enable global variant man...

entry.title: In SAP Business One, what is the purpose of the Order Number field in the Accounting tab of purchase
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

In SAP B1, on purchase forms like Purchase Quotation and Purchase Order, there is a field called Order Number in the Accounting tab.

I would like to know:

  1. How is this field populated?
  2. Does it have any impact elsewhere in the system?
  3. Is it possible to generate reports using this field?

entry.title: removing third digit decimal value
entry.content: Hi I getting source value weight as 13.777, I want the weight value as 13.77. can anyone help what is the logic need to be used

entry.title: Re: 关于生产核算3F0的科目如何在财务报表中配置
entry.content: 那是不是说明我需要对生产相关的科目单独创建。例如工资，需要创建生产人员工资科目，用于记录日常的FI工资分录并且成本中心选择生产部门，这样在报工或做实际人工费计算的时候通过CO凭证把工资转移到生产订单上，保证生产部门零余额。那对于这个生产工资科目，在FI角度，理论上应该和其他核算成本以及差异科目等初级要素合计为零。因此可作为暂时技术科目不放入利润表或一同放入利润表。不知道我的理解对吗？

Arvind

entry.title: Re: EWM SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, Warehouse Attr...
entry.content:
Hi,

The warehouse attributes can only be set once by our customer and cannot be changed anymore. So it needs to be very careful to select a correct UoM. If a wrong UoM is set (normally in the testing system), the customer normally copies another warehouse and set the UoM again. When it is the testing system and not so many movements, there is one option to open a ticket to ask the SAP team to change the UoM in the backend. Additionally, report needs to be run to correct the UoM of bin, HU, open WT...

The time unit I think is used in WT duration calculation (e.g. labor management). This is currently not available in the cloud WM.

entry.title: Re: Homogeneous DMO and DMOVE2S4
entry.content:
One query - is it mandatory to keep Migration server (AAS) on only windows server? What if my source system and target system would be on same Linux operating system, will DMOVE2S4 work in these scenarios? 

Regards,

Charanjit

entry.title: Re: UI5 Building Blocks: How to enable global variant man...
entry.content: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3242580

entry.title: Re: SAP RISE Migration Options for Partner
entry.content:
Hi Charanjit, in the context of RISE :
1. You will need to add an App server as part of source system in Migration VM before you start the conversion. The  SUM upon starting will detects that, if it is not running on the Primary Application Server (PAS) host, it offers the ASCS instance move. This will install the ABAP central services on the AAS in the target, and the previous PAS will not be started again after downtime.

2. You may refer to SUM DMO SAP Notes 3429205 - Central Note - Software Update Manager 2.0 SP21 [lmt_021] - SAP for Me

Please have take a look on the SUM DMO Page Software Update Manager and System Move: Switching the Operating System | SAP Help Portal

and About this Document | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: EHS Duplicate Resource Error in Identify Risk app
entry.content:
Hi @Ali_Kaynee,

I guess the system you are currently working on is either a development or test system - hence, we can safely try different things to get rid of the duplicate in the identify risks app.

Let's start with the "normal" process: Assigning the identified risk to a risk assessment project.

  1. Open the app "Assign Risks to Projects" (F2646),
  2. You should be able to see the risk(s) causing the problem in the list,
  3. On the upper-right corner, click on "Create Risk Assessment Project",
  4. A pop-up will show up. Enter the information about the risk assessment to be created: Assessment Type: JHA (Job Hazard Analysis), Job: General Engineer, Location: 10-A-8005 A, Operational Status: Normal Operation. The Job, Location, and Operational Status must match the one of the identified risk. Enter title as per your preference.
  5. In the list, select the "Corrosion" risk, and click on the button in the upper-right corner "Assign to Risk Assessment Project", pick the risk assessment project you created in step 4.
  6. Go back to the app "Identify Risks" (F2411), make sure that the risk is no longer shown.
  7. You can now open the created risk assessment project either from the app "My risk assessment projects" (F1999), or from the app "Manage risk assessment projects".
  8. In the tab "risks" you can either handle the risk as required, or set its status to "void".

The above steps describe the "normal" process of the risk assessment solution. Let's try these first (and ignore that there is an error), to get rid of the error in the first place. If the above steps either cause problems or don't solve the issue, I'd suggest to delete the identified risk (technically). To do so, please perform the following steps:

  1. In the backend, have a look at the table EHHSSD_RSK_ROOT. You can search by Location (technical name: LOC_ROOT_KEY_REF), Hazard (technical name: HAZARD), Job (technical name: JOB_KEY_REF), and Operational Status (technical name: OPERATION_STATUS) to find the duplicate risk.
  2. Once you found the duplicate risk, copy the content of the column NodeID (technical name: DB_KEY, the first column in the result list). It's a purely technical identifier, a so-called UUID and should look anything like this: 670D114......32FBE7.
  3. I'm using the following example: (it's not a job-based risk, but it does not matter for the following steps):
  4. Go to the transaction BOBT. Now, it will get a bit technical - but you can do it 😉,
  5. In the upper-left corner, there is one input field, enter EHHSS_RISK and hit enter,
  6. Below the input field, there is a little button with a folder icon, click on it, and pick By Key -> ROOT,
  7. In the pop-up box, enter the key you copied in step 2, click on ok
  8. On the right-hand side of the screen, the risk is loaded. Select it from the list (a), click on the little edit button (b), click on the delete button (c), and finally, save (d).
  9. Check the app "Identify Risks", the risk(s) should no longer be visible:

     

Please let me know if the above suggestions/steps solved your issue.

With kind regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: In SAP Business One, what is the purpose of the Order Number field in the Accounting tab of purc
entry.content: Hi there! In SAP Business One, the Order Number field in the Accounting tab of Purchase Quotation and Purchase Order forms is typically populated manually by the user. This field is used to reference external order numbers provided by customers or vendors. Yes, this field can impact other areas of the system, such as linking purchase orders to sales orders or tracking specific transactions. And yes, you can generate reports using this field. You can use SAP Business One's Query Wizard or Query Generator tools to create custom reports that include the Order Number field.

entry.title: Re: RFC Connection Error
entry.content: It seems that your issue is similar to the one described in the question, "RFC failure from Commerce Cloud to On-Premise ECC" and I believe the solution I provided for that question should resolve your issue as well. Please give it a try and let me know if it works.

entry.title: ABAP Dump Error
entry.content:
Hi Mates,

An issue has occurred where a change made by someone in the ABAP team to a standard material-related object has resulted in dumps across all material-related transactions. We need guidance on how to identify the root cause of the error and the best approach to rectify it without impacting the system further. Any suggestions or best practices to resolve this efficiently would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: CDS Views 7.5x Quick Reference
entry.content:
Introduction

This Quick Reference is a collection of new CDS functionality made available in ABAP 7.5x releases. This functionality is summarised in a way that will make sense to those familiar with SAP CDS. Links to the SAP Help  are also provided throughout to allow the reader to dive in deeper if desired. The version the functionality was released is also given to aid in determining if it is available in your system.

Contents

  1. CDS View Entity

  2. CDS Abstract Entity
  3. CDS Custom Entity
  4. CDS Projection views

 

1. CDS View Entity (7.50)

Syntax

[@entity_annot1]
[@entity_annot2]
...
[@view_entity_annot1]
[@view_entity_annot2]
...
[DEFINE] [ROOT] VIEW ENTITY view_entity
        [parameter_list]
         AS select_statement [;]...

Description

CDS view entities are non DDIC-based views. While they were introduced in 7.50 the old DDIC-based views (DEFINE VIEW ddic_based_view) only became obsolete in 7.56.

 

Extensions

EXTEND VIEW ENTITY (7.55)

Makes it possible to add new view fields to existing CDS view entities and CDS projection views by using CDS view entity extensions.

EXTEND VIEW ENTITY cds_view_entity | projection_view
       WITH
            [association1
             association2 ...]
            { select_list_extension }
            [GROUP BY field1, field2, ...  ]
            [EXCEPT | INTERSECT | UNION [ALL]  { ... }] [;]

 

ANNOTATE ENTITY (7.53)

Define CDS metadata extensions(type:DDLX) for any CDS entities with the exception of CDS table functions.

@Metadata.layer: #CORE|#LOCALIZATION|#INDUSTRY|#PARTNER|#CUSTOMER
   [@entity_annot1]
   [@entity_annot2]
   ...
   ANNOTATE ENTITY cds_entity WITH
     [ parameter_list]
     { [element_list] }[;]

 

Set Operators

UNION (7.56)       

Now supported in CDS view entities.

EXCEPT (7.56) 

Returns all rows of a SELECT statement that are not part of the result sets of the following SELECT statements.

INTERSECT (7.56)

Returns all distinct rows that are included in all result sets of multiple SELECT statements.

 

Functions

GET_NUMERIC_VALUE (7.56)

Conversion Function. Returns the numeric value of a currency or quantity without its currency or unit key.

 

  key carrid,
  key connid,
  key fldate,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'currency'
      price,
      currency,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'currency'
      paymentsum,
      GET_NUMERIC_VALUE( paymentsum )  / GET_NUMERIC_VALUE( price )
                as number_of_bookings

 

CURR_TO_DECFLOAT_AMOUNT (7.56)

 Conversion Function. Converts the function argument(must have data type CURR and a currency key ) into a type DECFLOAT34.

 

  key id,
      cuky                              as currency,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'currency'
      curr10_4                          as amount,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'currency'
      CURR_TO_DECFLOAT_AMOUNT(curr10_4) as curr_conv

 

SUBSTRING (7.57)

Function enhanced to accept literals, fields, parameters, expressions, and built-in functions as arguments pos and len.

SUBSTRING(arg, pos, len)     Substring arg from the position pos with length len. 

 

LEFT and RIGHT (7.57)

Functions enhanced to accept  literals, fields, parameters, expressions, and built-in functions as argument len.

LEFT(arg, len)                String of the length len with the len left characters of arg (ignoring the trailing blanks).

RIGHT(arg,len)              String of the length len with the len right characters of arg (ignoring the trailing blanks).

 

Types

Typed Literals (7.56) 

Now supported in CDS view entities. Not supported in the ON condition of a CDS association.

 

{
  key id,
      abap.char'aaa   '       as char_trailing_blanks,
      abap.sstring'aaa   '  as sstring_trailing_blanks,

      abap.string'aaa   '    as string_trailing_blanks,
      'aaa'                               as untyped_literal   }
where
  numc1 = abap.numc'1' and
   datn < abap.datn'20200101'

 

CDS Calculated Quantity (7.56)

A new type that is the result of a calculation using amount and/or quantity fields.

 

Application Session Variables (7.57)

In form $session.vname

vname

Value when accessed

bs_instance_id

Instance ID of the current business service instance. The returned value has the data type SSTRING.

bs_zone_id

Zone ID of the current business service instance. The returned value has the data type SSTRING.

 

Input Parameters (7.57)

abap.string now supported.

 

ABAP Reports

RUTDDLS_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES (7.56) 

Evaluates whether a migration from a CDS DDIC-based view to a CDS view entity is possible.

 RUTDDLSV2MIGRATION (7.56) 

Migrates CDS DDIC-based views to CDS view entities.

RUT_WHERE_USE_SQLVIEW (7.57)

Lists all repository objects that use CDS-managed DDIC views.

 

Annotations

Table Buffering Annotation (7.57).

Defines whether table buffering is allowed for a CDS view entity or not.

@AbapCatalog.entityBuffer.definitionAllowed: true|false

 

CDS entity buffer (7.57)

The actual buffering type can be defined by a separate ABAP tuning object called a CDS entity buffer with the DDL statement:

DEFINE VIEW ENTITY BUFFER ON cds_view_entity
                   LAYER CORE
                       | LOCALIZATION
                       | INDUSTRY
                       | PARTNER
                       | CUSTOMER
                   TYPE SINGLE
                      | {GENERIC NUMBER OF KEY ELEMENTS number}
                      | FULL
                      | NONE

Table buffering can be defined differently for the layers core, localization, industry, partner and customer.

 

Other

DISTINCT (7.56)

Available for SELECT statements in CDS view entities. Works like ABAP SELECT.

 

Reusing Expressions (7.56)

Reuse expressions defined in the SELECT list in other operand positions of the same CDS view entity.

 

key id                                 as field1,
    concat($projection.field1, 'x')    as field_reuse,

 

WHERE clause (7.56)

Now supports arithmetic expressions and case expressions as operands.

 

define view entity DEMO_SALES_ORDER_WHERE
  as select from
    demo_sales_order
    association to demo_sales_bupa as _partner
      on $projection.id = _partner.id
    {
      key so_key,
          id,
          company_code,
          _partner.family_name
    }
    where
          _partner.family_name like    'S%'
      and length( _partner.family_name ) = abap.int1'4'
      and created_on between abap.dats'20200101' and abap.dats'20200401'
      and cast( _partner.birth_name as DEMO_BT_BIRTH_NAME
                preserving type )  =       abap.char'Meier'

 

 HAVING clause  (7.56)

Now supports arithmetic expressions and case expressions as operands.

 

define view entity DEMO_CDS_VIEW_ENTITY_HAVING
  as select from sflight
  {
    concat_with_space(carrid, connid, 1) as ID,
    count(*)                             as col_count,
    seatsmax
  }
  group by
    carrid,
    connid,
    seatsmax
  having
    seatsmax > abap.int2'500'

 

Searched Case Expression (7.56)

Now supports arithmetic expressions and case expressions as operands.

 

define view entity demo_cds_searched_case_ve
  as select from
    spfli
    {
      key carrid,
      key connid,
          distance,
          distid,
          case
            when distance >= 2000 then 'long-haul flight'
            when distance >= 1000 and
                 distance <  2000 then 'medium-haul flight'
            when distance <  1000 then 'short-haul flight'
                                  else 'error'
          end as flight_type
    }
    where
      distid = 'MI'

 

2. CDS Abstract Entity (7.53)

Syntax

[@entity_annot1]
[@entity_annot2]
...
[DEFINE] [ROOT] ABSTRACT ENTITY abstract_entity
         [parameter_list]
          element_list[;]

Description

Works like a structure definition but nothing is created in DDIC and has annotations that can be evaluated with class CL_DD_DDL_ANNOTATION_SERVICE.

 

EXTEND ABSTRACT ENTITY (7.56)

Allows extension of existing abstract entity

EXTEND ABSTRACT ENTITY cds_abstract_entity WITH  { element_list_extension }

 

To-Parent Association Without ON (7.56)

The ON condition can be left out of a “to-parent” if the association target is also a CDS abstract entity.

 

define abstract entity DEMO_CDS_ABSTRACT_CHILD
{
  key key_field  : abap.char(1);
      data_field    : abap.int4;
      _to_parent  : association to parent DEMO_CDS_ABSTRACT_COMPOSITION  
                     on _to_parent.key_field = $projection.key_field;
           <- Last line can be omitted
}

 

 3. CDS Custom Entity (7.54)

Syntax

[@entity_annot1]
[@entity_annot2]
...
[@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy : 'class_name']
[DEFINE] [ROOT] CUSTOM ENTITY custom_entity
         [parameter_list]
          element_list[;]

Description

No DDIC entries created and has annotations that can be evaluated with class CL_DD_DDL_ANNOTATION_SERVICE.

Used in RAP as basis of unmanaged queries.

 

 EXTEND CUSTOM ENTITY (7.57)

Makes it possible to add new elements to existing CDS custom entities by using CDS custom entity extensions.

EXTEND CUSTOM ENTITY CustomEntity WITH { ElementListExtension }

 

4. CDS Projection Views

Syntax

[@entity_annot1]
[@entity_annot2]
...
[@proj_view_annot1]
[@proj_view_annot2]
...
DEFINE [ROOT] VIEW ENTITY projection_view
   [ PROVIDER CONTRACT provider_contract]               [From 7.56]
    AS PROJECTION ON cds_entity [AS alias_name]
   [association1 association2 ...]                                 [From 7.55]
   [redefined_assoc1 redefined_assoc2 ...]                    [From 7.56]
      { projection_list }
   [WHERE cds_cond]                                              [From 7.58 for Transactional Interfaces ]

Description

A special CDS view entity that is based on another CDS view entity (called projected entity). CDS projection views adapt a CDS data model for service-specific use cases.

 

Associations (7.55)

It is possible to add new, local, read-only associations association1, association2 to a projection view. Read-only associations in projection views can only be exposed but they cannot be used in path expressions to expose elements from new associations.

 

REDEFINE ASSOCIATION (7.56) 

For Projection views it is now possible to redefine an association. In this way, it is possible to modify an association from the underlying view. 

... REDEFINE ASSOCIATION [source.]_ProjAssoc [ filter] [AS _RedefinedName] redirection  ...

 

PROVIDER CONTRACT (7.56) 

For Projection views it is now possible to specify a provider contract specifies in which scenario a CDS projection view is used.  Provider contract options available:

  • TRANSACTIONAL_QUERY (7.56)             [Default if not provided]
  • TRANSACTIONAL_INTERFACE (7.57)    [Use for Transactional Interfaces]
  • ANALYTICAL_QUERY (7.57)                       [Use for Transient View Entities]

 

CDS Transactional Query (7.56)

Uses DEFINE [ROOT] VIEW ENTITY AS PROJECTION ON  with  provider contract “TRANSACTIONAL_QUERY”.

Transactional queries are intended for modelling the projection layer of a RAP business object. A transactional projection view must always be part of a business object. That means it must either be a root entity itself, or it must be part of a composition tree that contains a root entity

 

CDS Transactional Interface (7.57)

Uses DEFINE VIEW ENTITY AS PROJECTION ON  with  provider contract “TRANSACTIONAL_INTERFACE”.

CDS transactional interfaces serve as stable public interface layer in a CDS data model. They are typically used in the context of the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model to provide the basis for a RAP BO interface. 

WHERE  (7.58) condition allowed for Transactional Interfaces.

 

CDS Analytical Projection Views (7.57)

Uses DEFINE TRANSIENT VIEW ENTITY AS PROJECTION ON  with provider contract “ANALYTICAL_QUERY”.

A CDS DDL that is intended for modeling analytical queries within a CDS data model. A CDS analytical projection view defines an analytical query in its element_list.

The projected entity cds_entity can be an analytical cube view(7.57) or an analytical dimension view(7.58). One base view can serve as a basis for multiple analytical projection views.

entry.title: Re: SQL Server add link server and select data from SAP f...
entry.content: Dears， I write a ABAP program to call function SWNC_COLLECTOR_GET_AGGREGATES get the data, and dbco write to SQL Server table, It work. Best Regards, Eric Liao

entry.title: Re: Is BusinessObjects Still Relevant in 2025?
entry.content:
Dear Dallas,

I can totally support your view on this matter, when I see my Bobj customers to "copy" webi reports into the now more fancy MS Power Bi - I could roll my eyes... litterally 😉  - Well there are still enough Webi reports in our Repository - so one less - recreated painfully in PBI  - without standards -  like set in the BOBJ Universes - let the people play if they want to... MS Marketing is doing it's best. But  FUD is  a topic that counts - lately I met an ex colleague - he knew I am still working with BOBJ - "But isn't it "dead" already?" he asked me! ...  Well that hurts! I confess! 

Well for the next BOBJ Versions we have clear visions! Looking forward to BOBJ Future!

 

yours

 

WoBi

entry.title: Add new column in condition tab of sales order line item
entry.content:
Hello Team, 

There is a requirement of adding a calculation type on the line item condition tab of sales order. Is it possible to modify this screen? Please let me know, we have a reuirement for the same. 

Looking for your response. 

Warm Reagards, 

Rahul More

 

@Pricing

Regards

entry.title: Re: CDS Views 7.5x Quick Reference
entry.content:
Hi Community,

" CDS transactional interfaces serve as stable public interface layer in a CDS data model. They are typically used in the context of the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model to provide the basis for a RAP BO interface.  "

 

Thanks for your selfless sharing, it really help us a lot.

 

Regards,

🙂

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: Vendor master table

entry.title: Reversal of Work Order
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'd like to know how we do reversal of settlements when it comes to a Work Order.

Say a settlement has already been done and it generated an unwanted cost. Firstly, can i reverse these settled journals and if can, what will happen to the Work Order?

I was hoping to understand the correlation and the impacts of such situation.

entry.title: Re: Custom timeout page for Web Dynpro applications
entry.content:
Thanks Tudor.

That's brilliant!

entry.title: Re: RECADV not available in WE81
entry.content: I don't know what you want to achieve. You'd need to share more details.

entry.title: SAP GUI for Windows Keyboard delay
entry.content:
Hello everyone. We have a special problem with the SAP GUI for Windows. For some users, the keyboard input is sent to the screen field in the SAP GUI with a very long delay. To illustrate this again, here is a simple scenario. A shipment number is to be entered in the screen field in transaction VT03. The user types the number on his keyboard and after approx. 10 seconds it appears in the corresponding field in the SAP GUI.

The problem only occurs sporadically with some users. We have also noticed that this delay occurs with a time delay after restarting the computer. All users are using the SAP GUI for Windows 8.0 Patch 8. Changing the theme only delayed the occurrence of the problem but did not resolve it.

Has anyone noticed this problem and been able to fix it?

entry.title: SAP Hybris patch upgrade (from 2211.27 to 2211.28) causing API call response failure
entry.content:
As per product compatibility matrix, when we upgraded from SAP Hybris 2211.27 patch (with 2211.24 Integration Extension pack) to SAP Hybris 2211.28 patch (with 2211.26 Integration Extension pack), it's causing API call response failure.

Error sample:

[Request processing failed; nested exception is java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke \\\"com.xyz.facades.customer.data.Response.getProfiles()\\\" because the return value of \\\"com.xyz.facades.customer.data.Response.getResultSet()\\\" is null]

 

With same code, with 2211.27 patch, it works, but fails when used with 2211.28 patch. Can anyone help regarding this?

entry.title: Re: How to create custom form template (output management...
entry.content:
Hi @PrasanthPadmanabhan,

It seems like the form will be directly print.

Can it be possible to add preview function before print in the development?

If it can be, how can I develop preview function?

Best Regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: Re: ABAP Dump Error
entry.content:
Hello @KiranSAP3 

You need to analyze the dump. I'm pretty sure it will give you the answer. MARA does contain MATFI field in standard. Thus I guess it is just a matter of declaring variables in an ABAP program. 

I'd suggest to contact the ABAP team and ask them to either reverse the change or to rectify the issue. Why did they change SAP standard objects?

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: In me51n, fixed value field disable
entry.content:

In me51n, Fixed value field is disabled for particular user only. how I enable that field. I checked su53 & settings.

everything is fine.

entry.title: Re: Send purchase order automatically by email to one spe...
entry.content:
HEllo,

I figured this out myself.

In the tile (fiori) Output Parameter Determination, when selecting the rule for PO (in my case) and on the output type as determination step, it is possible to add specific supplier and/or excluded other in the business rules.

After redefining the values over all iFlows we would like to display just the custom status that are used.  

BR

entry.title: Re: removing third digit decimal value
entry.content:
Hi @Venkat060 ,

In model level in measures select data as Decimal and select places as 2

 as shown in picture

If you want in story level you can also do it in styling panel in Number format select decimal places

 

Kindly upvote and like

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Hana DB: Feature not supported on this instance
entry.content: In the Instance creation steps, no where it is giving an option for specifying a JSON. Would you please provide detailed steps.

entry.title: Re: How to use the multiselect combobox feature
entry.content:
Hi,

that’s correct, there’s a problem with the multicombobox that I have fixed in an upcoming update.

Meanwhile you can fix this by changing the formula to MAP(internalProps.tokenList, {token: item.token, selected: DEFAULT(FIND<existing>(privateVars.tokenStatusList, existing.token == item.token).selected, false)})

entry.title: Physical inventory whitout locking the whole storage bin?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
is there a way to perform a physical inventury e.g. per HU without locking the whole storage bin? (if you would build a automatic storage type as a 1-bin-black-box).?

 

br, doepat

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Content NetworkPackage Transport via BTP Transport Management service.
entry.content:
@sharmaar 

Hi Aravind,

cTMS is used for transport between tenants. Within the same tenant, users can manually import by choosing different space locations.

Thanks,

Ashok

entry.content: @NaveenKumarCThere is nothing on the screen. It just stays as is withn the characteristics area blank.

entry.title: Material master migration using LTMC
entry.content:
Hello,


Good day.


I know that we can create a new material master using the LTMC process, however I wanted to know if LTMC provides the flexibility to change the existing view of a material master which is already created in SAP.


 


Best PERSON_NAME,


Anup PERSON_NAME


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Material master migration using LTMC
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thanks for contacting SAP.

The Migration Cockpit is built to initially migrate data. It's not a mass maintenance tool and not built to be used as an interface to permanently upload data into the system.

To update and change an existing record, a mass maintenance and/or processing app available for the Migration Object must be used. This is the standard behavior.

Best regards,
Ron
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How to select combinable roadmaps
entry.content:
Hello @maheshmohan ,

As of now, Ariba is not available in combinable roadmaps.

Regards,
Sonam

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Content NetworkPackage Transport via BTP Transport Management service.
entry.content:
Hi Tomas  @tomas_balcar4 

Recently Transport monitor functionality is introduced. (2024.25 Release)

All the imports triggered via cTMS can be monitored here

Transport > Monitor 

Thanks,

Ashok

entry.title: Re: ECN API error: Unable to load ECNs of the current instance.
entry.content: Any luck with this? It seems to be this way from past several weeks? Is someone from database team looking into it?

entry.title: Re: CAll SAPMSSY0 transaction / screen 0120 through BDC when creating a vendor
entry.content: Thank you very much! I had a lot of trouble getting the recording code for that program and dynpro. SAP note 390084 provided a tip, but it wasn't enough, as it didn't mention the important OKCODE = 'PICK' or the CURSOR. This post really helped me! I needed to modify a "PA30" recording by adding this. Keep in mind that in my case, the list displayed only had two columns. However, to select the first column of the first row, I had to use "04/02" in the CURSOR instead of "01/01" (for the second row, "05/02", and so on). "04/01" didn’t work. So, I confirm that this solution worked in a SAP HANA system. Grettings from Perú

entry.title: SAP CodeJam in Vienna 2025

we would like to host a SAP CodeJam in March this year.
The specifics are:

Location: Vienna, Austria
Preferred Topics:

  • Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
  • Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python

Date: 20th March 2025

A location is organised, we will provide breakfast and lunch.

If you need further information please contact us at hello@sit.wien

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: 视频数据故事（Video Data Story），在SAP分析云中全新的数据展现方式
entry.content:
在SAP分析云最新的QRC1版本中，将会推出一个全新功能，视频数据故事（Video Data Story）。这个新功能将支持用户从现有的用户故事中直接设计并创建一个短视频，而不需要任何视频编辑的专业知识。对于用户来说，短视频会成为一种全新的展示数据洞察的方式，相比于传统的用户故事，短视频的形式会变得更有吸引力。

您可以从以下链接，大致了解下短视频形式的视频数据故事的样式：

https://video.sap.com/media/t/1_bu06re47

这篇博文会简单介绍下如何在SAP分析云中设计并创建一个视频数据故事（以VDS作为简称）。

- 在故事中打开支持VDS的开关

在用户故事的设置中，会有一个新的开关来设置是否支持VDS。需要注意的是，该设置只支持优化故事体验的故事。

- 创建视频数据故事

在查看模式下打开用户故事，在图表微件的上下文菜单中，会有一个新的选项，允许用户将选中的图表添加进一个新的VDS。

在当前版本中，目前只支持将部分的图表类型添加到VDS，包括：

柱状图、折线图、饼图、数据点图。

除了以上的四种图表类型，还支持将文本微件也添加进VDS。

当一个图表被添加后，一个新的VDS的设计面板会出现在屏幕的底部，用于展示当前VDS的概览及后续的设计。

在设计面板中，被添加的图表会单独显示，并展示动画效果，用户可以大致了解该图表在视频中的呈现样式。

重复以上步骤，可以添加更多的图表进VDS，之后用户也可以通过拖拽的方式来调整不同图表的先后顺序。

- 视频数据故事的相关设置

除了添加图表和调整相关顺序，用户可以对VDS做出一些额外的设计，从而可以设计出更个性化和有吸引力的视频。

  • 为图表添加标题和描述

更新完的标题和描述，也会在图表的预览图上同步展示。

  • 视频的朝向

在当前版本中，提供两种不同的朝向，来适配竖屏和横屏的不同场景。

  • 视频的整体标题

用户可以自定义视频的整体标题，标题将作为视频的片头首先展示。

  • 背景图片

用户可以为视频添加背景图片，来美化最终的视频。在系统中提供了一些预设的图片以供用户选择，同时用户也可以定义图片的明亮程度。

用户还可以选择自选的图片作为视频背景，图片可以从本地设备中上传。

  • 背景音乐

类似于背景图片，用户也可以选择为视频添加背景音乐来丰富视频的内容。系统也提供了多种不同的预设音乐片段以供用户选择。不同的是，目前还不支持自定义的背景音乐。

  • 图表之间的转场效果

对于两个相邻的图表，如果两者之间的数据有相互关系，用户可以添加特定的转场效果，来强调两者之间的关系。举例来说，用户先添加了一个柱状图到VDS，之后在同一个柱状图中，选择某个成员做下钻或排序，并将下钻或排序后的结果也添加到VDS中。在这种情况下，这两个图表之间是有相互关系的，用户则可以决定在这两个图表之间添加智能转场效果。而不是简单的对两个图表做分别展示。

- 保存并管理视频数据故事

当用户完成相关的设计后，可以将该VDS保存下来。

而VDS会在SAP分析云中作为一个新的文件被保存，之后用户就可以通过文件夹来对其进行管理。类似用户故事，一个被保存的VDS可以被修改、复制，或者共享给其他用户或用户组。

- 查看并下载视频数据故事

通过文件夹，用户可以以查看模式打开一个VDS，视频会在浏览器中直接播放。

用户也可以决定是否要将视频下载到本地。视频的生成会在后台完成，并通过SAP分析云内置的通知告知用户视频已经可以下载，并提供下载链接。下载完的视频会以.webm文件的格式被保存在本地。

 

以上就是对视频数据故事的简单介绍，未来也会对该功能做更多的功能增强，敬请期待。

entry.title: Re: Error While Trying to take Control of Query Manager M...
entry.content:
try this:

SAPbouiCOM.Matrix OMatrix= (SAPbouiCOM.Matrix)frmQuerymanage.Items.Item("7").Specific;

entry.title: Re: Commericial and free tier service switches and entitlements for partner
entry.content: I try to ask in another way. As far as I know free tier subscription is limited to 1 subscription in a PAYG global account. If I update the Process Automation subscription from free plan to standard (https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/upgrading-from-free-plan-to-standard-plan?locale=en-US&q=free+tier) in our partner global account do I have the free tier ressources again to setup a new free plan subscription in another subaccount for other demo cases? BR Reiner

entry.title: Re: How to add more records to an existing sap standard s...
entry.content:
• Did you get an error in the IDOC status log field PROTOCOL
  • Did you set the Have_to_change field to 'X', did the execution change it to 'E' (attempt was made to insert segment)
  • Where did you try to insert new segment(s) after an E1KNVVM segment or after an already existing E1KNVIM segment

entry.title: Re: ABAP Dump Error
entry.content: Thanks for the clarification Dominik Tylczynski They made changes in the BAdI enhancement but later reverted those changes. However, it is still resulting in a dump. Is there any alternative way to resolve this issue, such as restoring it back to the standard state?

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter January 2025
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here an overview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Preview available live sessions on SAP Support Portal, tailored per region:
APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America*

Streamline Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Innovations and Updates

Are you currently running SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and would like to stay up to date with the latest innovations and changes related to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition updates and releases? Join our new Meet-the-Expert virtual live session in January.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Release Assessment and Scope Dependency Tool

SAP Datasphere Fundamentals

Boost your SAP Datasphere project by joining one of our new Expert-guided Implementation virtual workshops. Through the course of these enablement sessions, we help you understand key concepts and see a quicker time to value when adopting SAP Datasphere. Now, available in Spanish, Portuguese, and Chinese variants along with the already running English language sessions.

SAP Datasphere Fundamentals

SAP Application Lifecycle Management Schedule: January 2025

Watch out our SAP ALM schedule for with a wide list of Expert-guided Implementation (EGI) session upcoming in January 2025. We have seven sessions around SAP ALM and SAP Cloud ALM. So, don’t miss getting yourself ready on all you need to learn around SAP Application Lifecycle Management.

See the full schedule: All you need to know about SAP Cloud ALM

Make yourself ready to take the next step on your SAP S/4HANA conversion

Each month we offer interactive live trainings on five topics related to SAP S/4HANA. The sessions are short, cover all relevant facts, and are offered in several languages.
Find out why in 2024 over 400 customers benefitted from this learning opportunity.

Pick the topic most relevant to you and register today

Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What’s New in Q4 2024

In the Q4 2024 update, we've introduced three new product pages for SAP Business Technology Platform Kyma Runtime, SAP Mobile Services, and SAP Translation Hub. Learn more about the latest features and enhancements.

Read the blog to learn more and stay tune for what’s coming in 2025

Expert-guided Implementation Pilot on Prerequisites for Joule Activation

Guiding customers to start using Joule and reinvent their daily interactions with SAP systems and applications today.

Contact us to join!

Why Have Over 20,000 Users Chosen SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps?

SAP Enterprise Support value maps offer structured, self-paced learning to help you find what you need, exactly when you need it. With direct access to SAP experts through collaborative forums, you’ll receive personalized guidance throughout your SAP journey. Included with every SAP cloud subscription, this expert-curated content and advisory resources are designed to help you get started, stay up to date, and consistently drive value for your business.

Sign up now | Watch video 

New dates for SAP Build Process Automation EGI Workshop

Register to this multiday workshop on Jan 27-31 & Feb 10-14 to understand and adopt SAP Build Process Automation. The deep-dive session showcases the capabilities of SAP Build Process Automation and how to create workflows, decision management, and task automation. Discover intuitive no-code capabilities through the configuration of a specific business process to approve sales orders automatically.

Join here: SAP Build Process Automation

Register Now for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!

Join us for an exclusive opportunity to explore the upcoming innovations of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release, including AI-driven features and Joule advancements. From January 21 to January 24, we invite you to 21 live sessions offering expert demos, insider previews, and real-time Q&A. Register now and stay on top of the cloud ERP landscape. 

Register here

How AI-Driven Recommenders Elevate Support Experiences

SAP’s Customer Support uses Incident Solution Matching (ISM), an AI-integrated service based on machine learning and large language models (LLM) – to improve its Real-Time Support capabilities that address a heterogenous customer base. The enhanced recommendation system allows SAP users to resolve their issues more efficiently by reducing downtimes and times to resolution, while increasing productivity.

Read the latest Forbes article from Stefan Steinle, head of Customer Support & Cloud Life Management

SAP Change Makers: Inside SAP's Mission Control: Protecting businesses when it counts!

In this episode of SAP Change Makers, host Oliver Huschke is joined by Vivian Luechau-de la Roche, Senior VP and Global Head of SAP Mission Control Center & Mission Critical Support. Together, they dive into the vital role of SAP’s Mission Control Center, how it ensures smooth business operations during critical moments, and how AI-driven innovations like PARIM (Proactive Automated Risk Identification and Mitigation) help prevent issues before they escalate. Learn how Mission Critical Support keeps businesses operational when it matters most.

Start the podcast

SAP’s Holiday Season Readiness Program: How SAP Helps Get Your Presents Under the Tree on Time

How can businesses keep their systems stable during peak-season sales, for example, during Black Friday Cyber Monday or other holidays? SAP's Holiday Season Readiness Program (HSRP) addresses these challenges by maximizing the scope of SAP Commerce Cloud for holiday campaigns. The program also uses proprietary AI-integrated tools to identify risks early and solve problems before they occur. Stefan Steinle, EVP and Head of Customer Support & Cloud Lifecycle Management, talks about how SAP works behind the scenes to achieve 100% uptime for their customers, with their predictive, proactive, and bi-directional support model.

Click here

Ignite your ALM Story! Register today for SAP ALM Summit NA 2025

Join the SAP ALM Summit NA 2025 in SAP’s headquarter in Newtown Square, Philadelphia. Learn about business transformation enabled by SAP Cloud ALM, SAP LeanIX, and SAP Signavio and find out how SAP guides customers to adopt clean core principles to be able to stay current with the constant flow of innovation.

Find all event registration details here

 

  • Guidance on signing up
  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy Video
  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy at a glance
  • Direct access to SAP Learning Hub (Login with your s-user ID and password)
  • SAP ES Academy Entitlement document
  • FAQ
  • SAP Enterprise Support value maps
  • SAP Support Portal
  • Basics: Sign up | Time zone setup |
  • Formats: EGI | MTE | Program
  • Search: Language | Keyword | Filter | Calendar
  • Upcoming live sessions in your region: APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America

entry.title: Re: MDG-BP replication to different receiving systems bas...
entry.content:
You'll need two models, one for RECEIVER1, the other one for RECEIVER2.

Then you can define the object and segment filters as mentioned by Saravanan.

entry.title: Existing RFC for Process Director, particularly for accessing data related to the /cockpit/1 t-code
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Existing RFC /Odata for Process Director, particularly for accessing data related to the /cockpit/1 transaction in SAP. Specifically, we’re looking to retrieve workcycle data, company code, vendor code details using the Process Director Document Number.

entry.title: Re: Is there option to avoid the popup No routing found while creating production order
entry.content: In snote 3528395 it mentioned that The "production scheduling profile" (if used) can determine the order type, which can contain the 'selection ID', which in turn determines the routing to be used. Now pay attention to the production versions in which the appropriate routing is maintained. During planned order conversion, the system searches for the routing and BOM with the production version specified in the planned order. If no production version (in which the appropriate routing is maintained) do not match the production version in the planned order, the system issues a dialog box stating that "No routing found" (program 'SAPLCOSD', screen '310').

entry.title: Re: How to access Cloud Foundry Controller API?
entry.content:
Hi @mike_zaschka,
hi all  and a Happy New Year 2025!

We have been struggling with this topic, as well, with some “sleepless nights” and accidentally found this community post and we are very happy to say, that you have provided this information to us – thanks.

Now, with providing a valid user, configured in the BTP (email / password) we have managed to get valid access-tokens.

Because your answer here is 3 years old, maybe you (or some one else in here) can give information, if accessing the Cloud Foundry can be managed differently. If we rely on this approach, this would mean, we would need some kind of a technical user on the BTP platform and from security perspective, can we some avoid providing passwords in plain text?

Thanks a lot in advance,

M. Spyker

entry.title: Add Event Types for Events Leading to Termination in Business ByDesign
entry.content:
Is it possible to add more Event Types to the Events Leading to Termination list?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Add Event Types for Events Leading to Termination in...
entry.content:
As per the standard system behavior, only two Event Types are available: Resignation and Dismissal.

Adding more Event Types is a new requirement, we recommend posting your query in the Influence Forum via https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/886.

entry.title: Re: Can the 2021 Fiori APP: Capacity Scheduling Table do these CM25 functions?
entry.content: Hi Ann,

entry.title: Re: Can the 2021 Fiori APP: Capacity Scheduling Table do these CM25 functions?
entry.content:
I was running into similar problems, when trying to use the fiori capacity planning table.
Did you find a solution in the meanwhile for both functionalities in the meanwhile? 

 

Best regards 

Felix

entry.title: Is it Possible to create a column in Variable Pay that can distinguish between Percentage and Money?
entry.content:
Hi Colleagues,

 

I'm facing a proyect where the client have a business goal that reflects a percentage. However, we have created custom columns to reflect the right money symbol for the minimum, target, maximum, obtained....

The thing is that in those columns I cannot have the business goal that show us the % symbol... Here is a screenshot for more information:

 

 

Is there a workaround? Thanks a lot, all the ideas are welcome guys!

@xavierlegarrec

entry.title: Why we have values in GR/IR account.
entry.content:
It is required by the financial standards, at the end of every FI period, the GR/RI accounts have to be split into two accounts

GR/IR Classification

  • Goods Received but not Invoiced} it is under liability
  • Goods Invoiced but not received} it is under Asset

In Sap the transaction posted for GR/IR Classification will go through all the open PO and check why we have balance.

The GR/IR Analysis/Maintenance transaction is run to write off the quantity remains in expense(loss) account.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition for Service | 2023 FPS1 Product Update
entry.content:
Service Request is supported in S/4HANA Private Cloud, not in Public Cloud

Regards, Gert

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA-Migration: Implications for the Data & AI landscape (Blog Series Part 1)
entry.content:
With its Cloud ERP applications, SAP offers various SAP S/4HANA deployment options (private, public, or hybrid) tailored to meet each customer’s business needs based on criteria such as organization size, business maturity, process complexity, and existing SAP footprint.

Consider the following scenario which represents a significant portion of the customer base. Many customers are currently on a digital transformation step moving from ECC to SAP S/4HANA Cloud due to several reasons:

  • Upcoming mainstream maintenance
  • Pursuit of innovation (e.g. AI)
  • Industry-specific challenges
  • Operational efficiency
  • Complex system landscape and integration (no single source of truth)
  • Costs and time to market

This transformation is not just a technical upgrade but a strategic move to modernize operations, enhance agility, and position organizations for future success.

Organizations undertaking SAP S/4HANA transformation programs face several key challenges in modernizing their data and analytics backbone. These challenges can be categorized into four main areas (data, people, processes, technology):

1. Data

  • Key Question: How do customers integrate SAP S/4HANA data effectively with other sources and external data?
  • Challenge: Ensuring seamless integration of diverse data sets to create a unified, real-time data landscape that supports analytics and decision-making.

2. People

  • Key Question: How can customers drive agility by empowering all stakeholders?
  • Challenge: Enabling collaboration and agility by providing stakeholders (business and IT) with the tools, insights, and skills they need to leverage data effectively in their roles.

3. Processes 

  • Key Question: How do customers optimize and streamline business processes to leverage SAP S/4HANA's full potential?
  • Challenge: Aligning and reengineering business processes to take advantage of S/4HANA's simplified architecture, automation features, and real-time capabilities while minimizing disruptions and ensuring continuous improvement. Further organizations need to critically evaluate which legacy processes are essential for the new environment.

4. Technology

  • Key Question: How do customers modernize their data and analytics backbone while building on existing systems?
  • Challenge: Leveraging existing technology investments and integrating advanced capabilities to modernize the data infrastructure, ensuring scalability and efficiency.

Addressing these challenges requires a holistic approach that aligns data, people, processes and technology strategies to achieve successful SAP S/4HANA transformation and unlock the full potential of modern analytics.
Organizations must recognize that the quality of their data underpins the effectiveness of AI, analytics, and decision-making processes.

Because companies that take AI seriously must take data seriously!

Consequently customers need to reconsider their data and analytics strategy, raising the question of what SAP S/4HANA offers in this domain.

The transformation brings several challenges, with the top five being:

  • The new processes and data structures within S/4HANA
  • Data migration & data mapping
  • Adopting modern data warehouse concepts (e.g. data mesh, data fabric)
  • Integrating external data
  • Leveraging embedded analytics

These challenges are closely interconnected, but this blog will specifically focus on the “Leveraging embedded analytics” which is followed by other editions as part of the blog series.

Embedded Analytics defines the integration of end-to-end analytics and real-time decision-making capabilities in SAP S/4HANA.

One of the significant advantages of S/4HANA is its seamless combination of transactional and analytical data within a single system enabling:

  • configurable KPIs 
  • role-based “Overview Pages” and cockpits
  • insight to action capability: analyze and resolve
  • historical data for simulations and predictions
  • flexible drill down for maximum transparency
  • optimize business processes in ERP by deeply integrating operational analytics data

The following screenshot from a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, retail, fashion, and vertical business home screen represents some of the available insight cards as part of the embedded analytics:

 


These insights also cover end-to-end processes, such as the three examples below, from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, retail, fashion, and vertical business system:


Before we move on it's important to clarify what embedded analytics specifically means in the context of SAP S/4HANA.

Embedded Analytics in SAP S/4HANA is designed to provide real-time insights directly within the system, eliminating the need for separate analytical platforms. The term “embedded” encompasses the following aspects:

Embedded at the Database Level

  • There is no distinct storage for analytical data; instead, the analytical data is drawn directly from transactional data in real time.
  • Access is provided exclusively to live data, ensuring up-to-the-minute accuracy.
  • Necessary data transformations are carried out via the Virtual Data Model (VDM), simplifying the integration of analytics.

Embedded at the UI Level

  • The same User Interface (UI) technology, often even the same screens, is used to display both transactional data and analytical insights.
  • This seamless integration ensures users can work with analytics and operational data without switching systems or interfaces, using SAP Fiori as the primary platform.

Embedded into Business Processes

  • Analytics are deeply integrated into business processes with role-based access to relevant analytical artifacts, ensuring that users see only the information pertinent to their role.
  • Intent-based navigation is utilized, guiding users to take appropriate actions based on insights.
  • This enables the implementation of an insight-to-action workflow, empowering users to make informed decisions and execute them immediately within the same environment.

By embedding analytics across these layers, SAP S/4HANA provides a unified system that combines operational and analytical capabilities, promoting efficiency and data-driven decision-making.

Let's have a look into the technical components:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 


SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics is designed as a multi-layered architecture, integrating real-time analytics seamlessly into the system. This architecture consists of three interconnected layers, each playing a critical role in enabling advanced insights and decision-making directly within SAP S/4HANA.

1. Data Layer

The foundation of the architecture is the S/4HANA Tables, where core transactional and operational data is stored. These tables encompass essential business areas such as Finance, Production, Procurement, and Transactions. The data in this layer is accessed in real time, ensuring that all analytics are based on up-to-date information.

2. Semantic Layer

The Virtual Data Models (VDM), built using ABAP Core Data Services (CDS) views, bridge the gap between raw transactional data and meaningful insights. These models define both analytical and transactional queries, transforming the underlying data into a semantic structure that is easier to interpret. The VDM eliminates the need for data replication by providing real-time access to transactional data. It is the critical layer that ensures data from the Data Layer is structured and ready for use in analytics, forming the backbone for interconnectivity.

3. Analytic Layer

The Analytic Layer provides the tools and interfaces that enable users to interact with the insights derived from the data.

  • The SAP Fiori UI delivers an intuitive, role-based user interface for accessing dashboards, reports, and other analytical tools.
  • The SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), embedded within S/4HANA, offers advanced analytics, visualization, and data storytelling capabilities. This ensures that users can analyze, visualize, and act on insights within the same environment.

Interconnection Between Layers

These layers are tightly integrated to provide a seamless analytical experience. The Data Layer serves as the source of truth, ensuring real-time, accurate data. The Semantic Layer processes and structures this data, making it ready for analytics by leveraging VDMs. Finally, the Analytic Layer presents this information to users through Fiori dashboards and SAC visualizations, enabling insights-to-action workflows. Data flows between layers through federation or replication, ensuring that users always have access to real-time insights without the need for separate analytical systems.

This interconnected architecture ensures that SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics is not only robust and efficient but also highly user-centric, empowering business users to make informed decisions based on reliable, real-time data.

What does this mean for the end user?

There are two types of embedded analytics tools that empower end users to drive their business:

  • Tools to display for the business users
  • Tools to create and adapt for analytics specialists

Let me share some insights into the advantages of embedded analytics for business users:

SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics provides a comprehensive suite of tools for business users, enabling real-time insights and decision-making directly within the platform. The offerings include:

 

 

 

 

 

 

Further let me also share an overview of the advantages for an analytics specialist.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

In conclusion: SAP S/4HANA’s embedded analytics offers intuitive and actionable tools for business users and analytics specialists, seamlessly combining operational and analytical capabilities to drive better outcomes.

 

Lets make it concrete to the context of a retail value chain and the respective personas within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, retail, fashion, and vertical business system:

 

 

 

Let's pick one specific area as an example.

Value Chain: Store Commerce & Connectivity

Persona: Store Manager

Example:

The Store Manager can leverage multiple embedded analytics features to optimize store operations and enhance business outcomes:

  1. Goods Movement Analysis

    • Use Case: Store Manager tracks inventory flows such as stock transfers, receipts, and returns in real time. For instance, the manager can identify slow-moving items and prioritize their promotion or clearance, ensuring optimal inventory levels across the store.

  2. Sales Performance

    • Use Case: Store manager analyses the store’s sales performance, identifying top-performing products, underperforming categories, and trends in customer purchasing behavior. The manager can use this data to adjust product placement, refine sales strategies, and focus on high-demand products.

  3. Current/Upcoming Promotions

    • Use Case: Store Manager monitors the success of ongoing promotions and their impact on sales. By comparing promotion-driven sales against historical performance, the manager can evaluate their effectiveness and refine future promotional strategies. This enables the store manager to align inventory levels with anticipated demand and avoid stockouts during promotions.

  4. Upcoming Goods Receipt

    • Use Case: Store manager gains visibility into expected deliveries to prepare the store for incoming stock. This helps with resource planning, such as scheduling staff for restocking and organizing storage areas.

By utilizing these embedded analytics features, the Store Manager can make informed decisions to boost sales, optimize inventory, and enhance the overall customer experience, all while improving operational efficiency.

 

With that I want to close the first edition of my blog series around "SAP S/4HANA-Migration: Implications for the Data & AI landscape".

In my next blog I will focus on the differentiation when embedded analytics is most effective and when strategic reporting becomes more relevant.

Stay tuned.

entry.title: Re: How to authenticate Cloud Platform API
entry.content:
Hi @mike_zaschka,
hi all  and a Happy New Year 2025!

We have been struggling with this topic, as well, with some “sleepless nights” and accidentally found this community post and we are very happy to say, that you have provided this information to us – thanks.

Now, with providing a valid user, configured in the BTP (email / password) we have managed to get valid access-tokens.

Because your answer here is 3 years old, maybe you (or some one else here) can give information, if accessing the Cloud Foundry can be managed differently. If we rely on this approach, this would mean, we would need some kind of a technical user on the BTP platform and from security perspective, can we some avoid providing passwords in plain text?

Thanks a lot in advance,

M.Spyker

entry.title: Re: How to add new filter definition in B1if 1.0
entry.content:
finally find the place, it is in the Package Design -- Definition -- Criteria Fields.

Hope this will help others.

entry.title: Unable to install Desktop Agent 3 in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am doing the tutorial Install and setup Desktop Agent 3 -  https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-setup-desktop-3-0-agent.html.

In Step 2, point 1, when I go to the Control Tower tab and select the option Agent Update, I see that its blank as shown in the below screen. I don't see, "Enter Secret ID to configure agent update". I am an S user and hence chose the option Automatically.  Can you please help in fixing this issue.

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Annual Sales List - Belgium
entry.content:
Hello Vanessa,

Please consider that:

  • The threshold of 250 EUR is indeed only valid for invoices.
  • In case of credit notes, any amount must be reported, an amount above 250 EUR as well as an amount under 250 EUR.

Best regards,

Agnes

entry.title: Re: QM - Quality management Artificial Intelligence (AI) Scenarios required
entry.content:
Hi Sundar,

I am not aware of an AI in the QM module but there is Visual Inspection combined with AI software in SAP DMC (Digital manufacturing Cloud). The scenario we tested was a product that got a picture taken which was compared with pictures in a database. The AI software concluded if the product could be accepted or proposed a non-conformity. As in-process quality inspection (origin 03) could be integrated with SAP DMC for data collection this could be an AI scenario. Note the AI software is not included in SAP DMC but a third-party software product.

Greetings, Herman
